brief_justification: Identifies LLN precisely with both weak/strong forms and correct application to all three examples; goes three levels deep — formal definition, per-example derivation, and CLT companion explaining sharpness of macro-determinism; no explicit monitoring of reasoning process, no named biases or mid-analysis course-corrections; examples illustrate rather than verify — no counterfactuals, boundary tests, or re-derivation from a different angle; presents conclusions with mathematical confidence but never flags assumptions (e.g., iid requirement, independence of decay events) or states what would invalidate the answer; mentions CLT as companion but does not evaluate ergodic theory, ensemble averaging, or other candidate principles as alternatives; every conclusion traces to a formal probabilistic statement or numerical derivation a third party could reproduce independently.
